verses.text: To the priests: if they will not 'give glory unto my name,' He will curse their blessings. Warning to leaders. Those who serve at the altar bear a heavier responsibility.
To the priests: if they will not 'give glory unto my name,' He will curse their blessings. Warning to leaders. Those who serve at the altar bear a heavier responsibility.
To the priests: if they will not 'give glory unto my name,' He will curse their blessings. Warning to leaders. Those who serve at the altar bear a heavier responsibility.
His covenant with Levi was 'of life and peace'; the true priest had 'the law of truth' in his mouth and turned many from sin. The ideal. A faithful minister walks with God and wins others to Him.
His covenant with Levi was 'of life and peace'; the true priest had 'the law of truth' in his mouth and turned many from sin. The ideal. A faithful minister walks with God and wins others to Him.
His covenant with Levi was 'of life and peace'; the true priest had 'the law of truth' in his mouth and turned many from sin. The ideal. A faithful minister walks with God and wins others to Him.
'The priest's lips should keep knowledge'; but these departed and made many stumble, so God made them contemptible. Fallen guides. Teachers who corrupt the way lose all honour.
'The priest's lips should keep knowledge'; but these departed and made many stumble, so God made them contemptible. Fallen guides. Teachers who corrupt the way lose all honour.
'The priest's lips should keep knowledge'; but these departed and made many stumble, so God made them contemptible. Fallen guides. Teachers who corrupt the way lose all honour.
Have we not 'all one father'? Why deal treacherously? Judah married the daughter of a strange god. Mixed marriages. To join with idolaters is to betray the covenant family.
Have we not 'all one father'? Why deal treacherously? Judah married the daughter of a strange god. Mixed marriages. To join with idolaters is to betray the covenant family.
Have we not 'all one father'? Why deal treacherously? Judah married the daughter of a strange god. Mixed marriages. To join with idolaters is to betray the covenant family.
They weep at the altar, yet God regards them not, for He witnessed their treachery 'against the wife of thy youth.' Tears unheeded. No worship can cover wrong done at home.
They weep at the altar, yet God regards them not, for He witnessed their treachery 'against the wife of thy youth.' Tears unheeded. No worship can cover wrong done at home.
God seeks 'a godly seed'; 'the LORD... hateth putting away,' so deal not treacherously. Marriage guarded. God hates the breaking of the marriage bond.
God seeks 'a godly seed'; 'the LORD... hateth putting away,' so deal not treacherously. Marriage guarded. God hates the breaking of the marriage bond.
They weary the LORD, saying the evildoer is good in His sight, 'Where is the God of judgment?' Cynical doubt. To call evil good provokes the God who will surely judge.
